name: input-otp
title: Input OTP
description: A one-time-passcode entry field built on Base UI's OTP Field. Renders a labeled role=group of N single-character inputs (default 6) that keep paste, SMS one-time-code autofill (autocomplete=one-time-code), and assistive tech fully working. The active cell is signaled by a focus ring plus border (not color alone), filled cells by a stronger border, and errors by a thicker inset ring, so state survives forced-colors mode. Numeric, alpha, alphanumeric, or unrestricted validation; optional masking; controlled and uncontrolled; onValueChange and onValueComplete; and Field wiring.
useWhen: Entering a short verification / one-time passcode sent by SMS or email (2FA, sign-in, confirmation)
A fixed-length code where per-character cells help users read and correct it
You want SMS-code autofill and full-code paste to fill every cell automatically
avoidWhen: Collecting free-form or variable-length text (use an Input)
A long secret such as a password (use an Input with type=password)
A single value chosen from a set (use a radio-group or select)
The code carries meaning beyond a fixed count of characters

keyboard: Tab moves focus into the field (the active cell); Shift+Tab moves away
Typing a character fills the current cell and advances to the next
Backspace clears the current cell and retreats to the previous cell
Arrow Left / Arrow Right move between cells (mirrored under dir=rtl)
Home or Arrow Up jumps to the first cell; End or Arrow Down jumps to the last filled cell
Delete removes the current character; Ctrl/Cmd+Backspace clears the whole field
Pasting a full code fills every cell at once
Disabled cells are removed from the tab order and cannot be edited
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (each cell is a 2.75rem square input)
notes: The container exposes role=group named by the required visible label via aria-labelledby; every cell inherits that name so a screen reader announces the field at each caret position.
Cells are real <input> elements, so full-code paste, SMS one-time-code autofill (autocomplete=one-time-code on the first cell), and assistive technology are never intercepted or blocked.

Non-color redundancy: the active cell shows a focus ring plus a color-shifted border, filled cells carry a stronger border, and error state adds a thicker inset ring — each is distinguishable in Windows High Contrast / forced-colors mode.
Every cell keeps a visible border in every state so forced-colors mode always paints a boundary.
inputMode follows the validation type (numeric by default; text for alpha/alphanumeric) so the correct virtual keyboard appears on mobile.
An optional description is linked via aria-describedby on both the group and each cell and excluded from the accessible name.
Direction-aware: wrapped in AmbientDirection so Arrow-key navigation and Home/End mirror under dir=rtl like the native components.
